answer: `FURNSH` loads SPICE kernels into a program, handling both binary and text kernels [file:../data/spice/src/spicelib/keeper.f:761-764]. It recognizes meta-kernel keywords like `KERNELS_TO_LOAD`, `PATH_SYMBOLS`, and `PATH_VALUES` to load multiple kernels [file:../data/spice/src/spicelib/keeper.f:808-849]. This routine provides a uniform interface for loading SPICE kernels [file:../data/spice/src/spicelib/keeper.f:1002-1005].
